1: Hi there, and welcome to SYNERGY Home Care of Denver. We are so glad you are here. Get comfortable. Take a breath. Let us walk through everything you need to know to feel ready and confident for your first shift.
2: First, let us cover who you will be working with every day. Our office is open Monday through Friday, eight in the morning to five in the afternoon. The main office number is 3 0 3, 7 5 6, 9 3 2 2. Save that in your phone right now if you have not already. After hours, that line is for emergencies only. Please call during business hours for anything that is not urgent. Your office team is Anysia, Kiaren, Kyra, and Jacqueline. You are going to get to know all of us pretty fast. We pick up the phone, we call you back, and we are on your side. Our mission is simple. We stay trusted by being compassionate and providing quality care, so our clients can stay in their own homes safely and independently for as long as possible. That is the whole job, right there.
3: We have got your back. I want you to hear that before anything else. Nobody is perfect. Not the office, not the clients, not the families. It really does take a village. We believe in you, and we see your potential. If something is not working out, or you are feeling burned out, please talk to us. We would much rather hear it early than late. Out in the home, you are our eyes and our ears. You will notice things we never will from the office. We count on that. One thing to remember. We are non medical caregivers. That means companionship, light housekeeping, and personal care. During your interview, we asked a series of yes or no questions. That is how we match you to the right clients. The better the match, the better the shift goes for everyone.
4: A few ground rules that keep everyone safe and successful. Be on time. Plan ahead. Check the address the night before. And remember, snow days are not a thing in this job. Our clients still need us when the weather turns. Stay off your cell phone during shift. Be proactive. Keep busy. There is always something useful to do. Do not overshare with clients. Keep the focus on them. No sleeping on shift, and that includes overnights. We do not guarantee or promise hours. Here is the deal. The more you give us, the more we give you. Consistency is the goal. We want to keep you with the same client as long as possible. That is better for them, and better for you. Bring your own meals and snacks for breaks and lunch. If a client offers, you can accept. Just do not expect it. No family, no friends, no pets on shift. And the big one. If something feels weird, or looks different, call the office. We mean it. We would much rather get a call about nothing than miss something that mattered.
5: Let us talk about how to show up looking the part. Scrub top and bottom, any color you like. Cartoons and patterns are fine. You can pick them up at Walmart, Family Dollar, Dollar Tree, Scrubs and Beyond, Crazy Scrubs, or Amazon. Whatever fits your budget. Tennis shoes or sneakers, closed toe. Wear your name badge every shift. If you are not vaccinated against the flu, wear a mask. No heavy smells. That means no cigarette smoke on your clothes. No strong perfume or cologne. Many of our clients are sensitive to that. Cover tattoos. Take out piercings and jewelry. Keep your nails short. Gloves are provided by the client in their home. And the most important part of the dress code, be approachable. A smile goes a long way.
6: Now the part everyone wants to know about. Payday. Pay is bi weekly, every other Friday. Direct deposit is strongly encouraged because it is faster and safer. You can pull up your pay stubs anytime by logging into TRION. And here is the good news. The better you do, the better the raise you will get at your annual review. We notice the caregivers who show up, document well, and take great care of their clients. That gets rewarded.
7: A few things we expect from you, and these matter. If you need to call off a shift, or you need to be removed from a shift, we need 24 hours notice. No texts. No emails. You have to call the main office number. That is the only way it counts. If you are running late, communicate. A quick phone call to the office goes a long way. Do not leave a shift early, and do not stay later than scheduled, without our approval first. If a client wants to change the schedule, ask them to call the office. Do not work that out on your own. And do not share your personal phone number with clients. Everything goes through the office. That protects you, and it protects the client.
8: Here is how scheduling works at our office. Schedules are released every Thursday at four thirty in the afternoon, and they cover the upcoming week, Monday through Sunday. As soon as yours posts, check the client addresses, read the care plan, and review the A D L's so you know what each shift looks like. If you have any questions, conflicts, or concerns about your schedule, call us on Friday. If we need to remove you from something, we will. We assign you to shifts based on your availability, and your preferences. Allergic to cats? Cannot drive clients? Not comfortable with incontinence care? Tell us. We listen. We try our best to keep you in your geographic area. We service clients twenty four hours a day, seven days a week. Shifts can range anywhere from one hour, to twelve hours. If your availability changes, let us know right away. And request your time off at least two weeks in advance, so we can plan coverage.
9: If you are ever injured or hurt on the job, please call the office to report it right away. That is what workman's comp is for, and we want you taken care of. Speaking of taking care of you, here is what SYNERGY provides. Major medical, dental, and vision are available for full time employees, one year after hire, with thirty hours worked consistently each week. Four oh one K is available to everyone at ninety days. We automatically enroll you with a three percent deduction from your paycheck, and you can opt out if you do not want it. We strongly encourage saving even a little. And remember this. You have to take care of yourself first, before you can take care of anybody else. You cannot pour from an empty cup. We mean that.
10: If you will be driving for a client, here is what we need from you. Keep your car clean, and your tank full. You never know when an errand will pop up. Follow traffic signs and laws. Stick to the speed limits. And seatbelts on, for everyone, every time. Make room in your vehicle for a walker or a wheelchair if your client needs it. Clients, family members, and friends, cannot drive you. You drive them, or they do not go. Track your mileage so you can claim it for reimbursement. And we need a current copy of your auto insurance on file with the office. If yours is about to expire, send us the new one before it does.
11: A few extra things you might not know about. You can work at more than one SYNERGY office at the same time, if that fits your life. Sometimes facilities near us borrow our staff. When that happens, remember, we are guests in their building. All eyes are on us. Stick to the dress code. Wear your badge. Build a good relationship with their staff. Document carefully. Take your breaks the right way. And protect our reputation. People remember the SYNERGY caregiver who did it right. And if you are interested, we also offer live in shifts, in addition to hourly. Talk to the office if that sounds like something you would want to do.
12: Before we wrap, a quick reset. We hope you are starting to feel ready for your first shift. We covered a lot. Take what you need and ask about anything that is not clear. If something feels off out there, call the office. That is what we are here for. Now, two more important things. Let us walk through the Axis Care app, and then memory care, because both of those will come up in your very first week.
13: The Axis Care app is the tool you will use every shift. Let us cover the most important parts. Care notes can be seen by everyone. The office, the family, and other caregivers. So please be polite, and stick to the facts. Document what you saw, not how you felt about it. Pictures help us document. Use them for any changes you notice in the client. For general documentation. And to capture receipts when you run errands. If you ever have trouble clocking in or clocking out, the most common fix is the server code. Our server code is 1 3 4 0 4. That is one, three, four, zero, four. It is also printed on the back of your badge, so you will always have it.
14: Clocking out is where a lot of new caregivers get tripped up. So listen close. There are three parts you have to complete, before you can fully clock out. Part one. A D L's. The activities of daily living checklist for that shift, all the way through. Part two. Notes. A short note about how the shift went. Anything you skipped. Or anything worth flagging. Part three. The change in condition question. Did you observe a change in your client's condition today? Yes or no. Answer it honestly. Once all three parts are done, then you can clock out. If you skip one, the system will not let you finish, and we will be calling you. Take the extra minute. It matters.
15: Last but not least, memory care. Some of our clients have memory loss, and caring for them takes a slightly different approach. These tips will save you on a hard day. Approach from the front, never from behind. You do not want to startle them. Live in their reality. If they think it is nineteen sixty two, you are in nineteen sixty two with them. Do not correct. Do not argue. Tell them your name. Then say, I am here to keep you safe. That phrase calms a lot of situations down. The magic words are, just for today. Just for today, we are going to take a shower. Just for today, we are going to have lunch. It works. Empower them with choices. Would you like the blue shirt, or the red shirt? Choices give back control. Have a thick skin. Sometimes they will say things that sting. It is the disease talking, not them. Distract. A snack. A story. A joke. A song. All of those are great tools. And redirect. Move them gently to something else, when something is upsetting them. Memory care is some of the most rewarding work you will ever do. Be patient with them, and be patient with yourself. That is all we have for now. Welcome to the team. Now go take great care of somebody.
